reasoning: Chrome has held a dominant market share (65-70%) globally for years, and data from mid-2025 confirms this position remains virtually unchanged. The nearest competitor, Safari, holds roughly 16-18% market share. Because the knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, there are only about seven weeks left before the resolution date at the end of 2025. Historically, browser market share moves extremely slowly, typical shifts being fractions of a percentage point per month. It is functionally impossible for Chrome to lose a ~50-point lead in under two months, regardless of any conceivable regulatory, competitive, or technical developments within that brief window. Applying the maximum allowed certainty confidence.
batch_queries: Will Google Chrome be the most popular browser at the end of 2025?
